_documentation:
HOW TO READ THIS FILE — Category policy for the sourcing heatmap.

• Each top-level key whose name does NOT start with an underscore (_) is one sourcing CATEGORY (e.g. IT Infrastructure).
• The underscore key _documentation is only for people; the app ignores keys starting with _ when listing categories.
• Inside a category you normally have:
    – default_preferred_status, category_strategy_sas, supplier_preferred_status → affect Strategic Alignment (SAS).
    – scoring_mix (optional) → how MUCH each factor matters when computing the 0–10 priority score for that category.
• scoring_mix has two blocks:
    – new_sourcing_request → weights for intakes / requests with no contract yet.
    – existing_contract_renewal → weights for renewals / existing contracts.
• Under each block, use the long field names (they include the metric code in CAPITALS).
• Numbers are shares of the formula (like percentages that should add to 1). If they don’t, the engine renormalizes.
• You can also add plain_english under scoring_mix: a short sentence for your team; it is not used in math.

scoring_mix.plain_english: Default balance for infrastructure: urgency and spend matter a bit more than category-wide spend shape.
Software: tilt toward financial impact and strategic fit on renewals; new requests still stress timeline and spend.
Hardware: emphasize supplier risk and concentration; useful when supply continuity is the main story.
